project: Flow Improvements for New Player Legibility
description: A collection of UX improvements to make the game less confusing for first-time hosts and players. Right now the game works, but new users hit friction points: unclear UI during recording/grading, Laravel boilerplate branding everywhere, the host can't actually play, invalid game codes blow up the page, and there's no way to review a finished game. These changes make the game feel like a real product instead of a dev prototype.

userStories.title: Remove host audio upload fallback from recording UI
userStories.description: As a host, I should not see a confusing "upload audio on behalf of player" option during a player's recording turn, because it makes me think I'm supposed to do something when I'm not.
acceptanceCriteria: The host audio upload button/form is completely removed from the Play page recording UI
The backend endpoint (host-upload-audio) can remain for now but the UI no longer links to it
Host sees the same "waiting for player to record" view as any other non-active player
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add grading wait state with spinner and status text
userStories.description: As a player who just finished recording, I want to see a clear indicator that my speech is being processed, so I'm not staring at a blank screen wondering if something broke.
acceptanceCriteria: After audio upload completes, the active player (and all other players) see a spinner/loading state
Status text updates through the pipeline stages: "Transcribing your speech..." then "Grading your explanation..."

The backend play-state polling endpoint returns enough information to distinguish between transcription-in-progress and grading-in-progress (use the PipelineLog timestamps: whisper_sent_at set but whisper_response_at null = transcribing; gpt_sent_at set but gpt_response_at null = grading)
When grading completes, the UI transitions to the results view without requiring a manual refresh
If grading fails, show a user-friendly error message instead of hanging on the spinner forever
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Allow host to play the game as a regular player
userStories.description: As a host, I want to be assigned turns and record speeches just like every other player, so I can actually play the game I'm hosting — especially important in 2-player games where the host is the only other person.
acceptanceCriteria: TurnAssignmentService::assignTurns() includes the host player when generating turns (currently filters to non-host players only)
Host is assigned topics they did not submit, same as other players
When it's the host's turn, they see the topic choice screen and then the recording UI on their device
Other players see the "waiting" screen while the host records
Host's speech is transcribed and graded identically to other players
Host accumulates score and appears on the leaderboard
The "advance to next turn" button still only appears for the host, but only AFTER their own turn's results are shown (not during their own turn)
A 2-player game (host + 1 guest) works correctly with both players getting turns
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add microphone permission explanation on join/lobby page
userStories.description: As a new player joining a game, I want to understand why the game needs microphone access before I'm suddenly prompted by the browser, so I'm not caught off guard.
acceptanceCriteria: On the join page (where the player enters their name), a short text block explains: "This game needs your microphone because you'll be giving an impromptu speech. We'll ask for mic access when it's your turn."
The text appears before/above the name input, clearly visible without scrolling
The explanation is brief (1-2 sentences max), not a wall of text
The browser's native microphone permission prompt is NOT triggered on this page — it should still only be triggered when recording actually starts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Inline error for invalid game codes instead of 404 page
userStories.description: As a player on the home page, if I enter an invalid game code and hit Join, I should see a friendly inline error instead of the entire page turning into a 404.
acceptanceCriteria: When a user submits an invalid/nonexistent game code on the home page, the page does NOT navigate away
An inline error message appears near the Join button (e.g., red-bordered div saying "We couldn't find a game with that code")
The error disappears when the user starts typing a new code
The join form validates the code via an API call (e.g., GET /games/{code}/exists or similar) before navigating
Valid codes still navigate to the join/lobby page as before
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove all Laravel Starter Kit branding
userStories.description: As a user on any page of the app, I should not see any Laravel branding, GitHub links, documentation links, or "starter kit" language. The app should feel like its own product.
acceptanceCriteria: No page anywhere in the app contains the text "Laravel" (except possibly in HTML meta generator tags or server headers, which users don't see)
No links to GitHub repos, Laravel documentation, or any framework docs appear in the UI
The navigation/header does not reference "Laravel Starter Kit" or similar
Any placeholder branding is replaced with the app name "How Does That Work?" or a neutral alternative
Footer links, if any, are relevant to the game (not framework boilerplate)
The login, register, and password reset pages are similarly cleaned up
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Show credit balance on settings/profile page
userStories.description: As a host, I want to see how many credits I have on my profile or settings page, so I know if I need to buy more before starting a game.
acceptanceCriteria: The user's credit balance is displayed on the profile/settings page
The balance is clearly labeled (e.g., "Credits: 12" or "Game Credits Remaining: 12")
If credits are zero, the display makes this obvious (not just "0" buried in a corner)
Optionally: a link or button to purchase more credits is shown nearby
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Game history review page for hosts
userStories.description: As a host, after a game is complete, I want to be able to go back and review all the transcripts, grades, and feedback from that game, so I can relive the fun or share highlights.
acceptanceCriteria: A new page exists at a route like /games/{code}/review (or similar)
Only the authenticated host of that game can access it (other players and unauthenticated users get a 403 or redirect)
The page displays every turn from every round: player name, topic, transcript of their speech, grade, score, and AI feedback
Turns are organized by round, in the order they were played
The page is accessible from a "game history" list somewhere in the host's authenticated area (e.g., dashboard or profile)
Completed games appear in a list with the game code, date played, and number of players
Typecheck passes
